steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is rhetorical synthesis using a data table. The decisive clue is the student's finding: tribal activity contributed over \$141,000 per employee — the answer must correctly rank this figure against the three other industries shown in the table.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• "above all three of the other industries listed in the table.": Transportation/warehousing contributes more per employee than tribal activity, so tribal activity is not above all three. ✗
  • "below transportation/warehousing but above both manufacturing and health care.": Accurately reflects the table: transportation/warehousing exceeds \$141,000 per employee, while manufacturing and health care both fall below it, placing tribal activity second among the four industries. ✓
  • "above either health care or manufacturing and nearly equal to transportation/warehousing.": "Nearly equal" misrepresents the data — the table shows a meaningful gap between transportation/warehousing and tribal activity, not near-equality. ✗
  • "below all three of the other industries listed in the table.": Both manufacturing and health care contribute less per employee than tribal activity, so tribal activity is not the lowest-ranked industry. ✗
That matches the correct answer.
